name: React Front-End Fundamentals
goal: Build a CRUD admin dashboard front-end for the sso_group_sync project (assumed scope; confirm with project owner). Includes JS/HTML/tooling prerequisites — Mark is at JS-zero per 2026-04-11 calibration.

history.notes: FAIL (borderline). Q1 (0.7): correctly identified no loops/conditionals/functions, but described HTML as 'manipulating elements within the tree' — load-bearing misconception. HTML IS the tree (declarative noun), JavaScript manipulates it (verb). Re-emphasize: HTML is data, JS is program, DOM is the live in-browser tree. Q2 (0.75): 3/4 correct (4 elements, parent=label, required=boolean) but DID NOT ANSWER (d) about which element is void — answer is <input>. Watch for incomplete answers on multi-part questions. Q3 (0.85): clean — correctly said it won't work, no variable assignment, will render literal string 'userName'. 'Kind of tool' answer was a python input() example which is wrong medium (CLI not web) but right intuition (need a real program with variables). Used as natural segue to JSX/React.

FAIL. Pre-retrieval revealed misconception MUTATED: now thinks 'JS translates HTML into DOM.' Re-taught: browser PARSER builds DOM from HTML at load time, JS arrives later and modifies the existing DOM. Mark confirmed (recipe/cake/person model: parser bakes the cake, JS is the person who modifies the cake). Conceptual frame now solid. Q2 (1.0): clean elaboration on live-clock impossibility using cake model. New misses are MECHANICAL/VOCABULARY: Q1 (0.5): undercounted elements (said 2, actual 5 — does not realize each nested tag pair counts as its own element); confused 'parent' with 'previous sibling' (named first as parent of second ; correct = the
  • directly containing it). Q3 (0.83): marked as PAIRED — it's VOID. Q4 (0.25): wrote instead of (invented tag); wrapped in

    despite 'single element' instruction; put class on wrapper not on image; admitted not knowing alt syntax (alt= was not in prior teaching, fair). Re-teach focus next session: (1) tree vocab — parent/child/sibling drilled with diagrams; (2) element counting — every opening+closing pair = one element; (3) tag specifically + alt attribute mandatory.

PASS (drill mode). 5/5 construction prompts clean, escalating from flat list to 4-level nesting (div > ul > li > img). Both yesterday's residual error modes vanished: nested link inside
  • closed cleanly with text + ;
    containers closed properly throughout, including the deepest prompt with img inside li inside ul inside div. Alt text present and meaningful on both images in #5 (no 'image' as filler). Took 4 sessions to clear html_basics — the breakthrough was switching from re-teach to drill format. Mastery 0.0 → 0.2, reps 0 → 1, next review 2026-05-07. NEXT: another short drill at next review to confirm retention; if it passes again, html_basics mastery climbs toward the 0.7 gate that unlocks downstream concepts (html_forms, jsx_syntax). Worth applying drill-mode lesson to other concepts in this domain when they reach the production-fluency stage.

FAIL (borderline, 3rd in a row). Pre-retrieval: cake/recipe/person model returned cleanly with two minor slips ('HTML is tree-like' — HTML is text, DOM is tree; 'JS allows us to run the DOM' — inverted, browser runs JS). Targeted re-teach went well: tree vocab check 3/3, element counting 1/1, void+img check 2/2. ALL micro-checks passed. Failure mode emerged in the FULL ASSESSMENT (construction tasks under recall). Q1 (0.5): forgot the
    wrapper despite prompt saying 'unordered list' — wrote loose
  • s; element count of 4 was internally consistent with what he wrote but missed the 5-element correct structure; also used 'href = ' with spaces (non-standard but not wrong). Q2 (0.6): gave fallback-on-image-failure reason but framed as DEVELOPER insight; completely missed ACCESSIBILITY/screen reader as the primary reason despite it being explicit in teaching. Q3 (1.0): clean — spotted and
    as invalid void closures. Q4 stretch (not scored): got 'alt is not a child' but didn't articulate that void elements have NO children at all and that 'A cat' is the attribute VALUE not a text node. PATTERN ACROSS 3 SESSIONS: micro-checks always pass, full-construction questions reveal that mechanical/structural pieces aren't load-bearing yet (forgets wrappers, forgets which audience an attribute serves, invents tags). Recommendation: stop re-teaching, switch to drill mode — short construction prompts back-to-back ('write the HTML for X, write it for Y, write it for Z') to build muscle memory. The conceptual scaffolding is fine; what's missing is fluent production. POST-ASSESSMENT MINI-DRILL (6 prompts, same session): scored ~0.85 — significant jump from the 0.70 formal assessment.
      wrapper appeared automatically on prompts 2 and 4 (the exact gap from Q1). New residual error mode: forgets closing tags on the MOST NESTED structures specifically — missed inside
    • and missed wrapping h1+p+a. Drill mode validated as the right intervention. NEXT SESSION (2026-05-04): another short drill, weight toward nested-structure prompts (multi-level containers, links inside list items, divs containing multiple children) to nail the close-tag discipline. If drill scores >= 0.8, treat as pass even if formal assessment style isn't run — production fluency is the actual goal here.

concepts.name: JS Variables (let, const, var, primitive types)
JS Functions (declarations, arrow functions, return values)
JS Array Methods (map, filter, reduce, forEach)
Rendering Lists (.map and the key prop)
useEffect Basics (dependencies, cleanup, when effects run)
Data Fetching in useEffect (loading, error, success)
Component Structure (file organization, when to extract)
API Integration Pattern (REST CRUD from React)
